questions.text:
A toy store gives each of the toys it sells a unique code. The codes are 3-digit hexadecimal codes, for example A4B. A computer is used to store a database of the codes. The computer has 12-bit registers.

Give the largest hexadecimal number that can be stored in a 12-bit register.

marking_points.text: FFF

questions.text:
A toy store gives each of the toys it sells a unique code. The codes are 3-digit hexadecimal codes, for example A4B. A computer is used to store a database of the codes. The computer has 12-bit registers.

Give the largest denary number that can be stored in a 12-bit register.

A toy store gives each of the toys it sells a unique code. The codes are 3-digit hexadecimal codes, for example A4B. A computer is used to store a database of the codes. The computer has 12-bit registers.

Two toys have the hexadecimal codes 429 and 1A3.
Convert the two hexadecimal codes to 12-bit binary numbers.

429
1A3

A toy store gives each of the toys it sells a unique code. The codes are 3-digit hexadecimal codes, for example A4B. A computer is used to store a database of the codes. The computer has 12-bit registers.

Two binary numbers that are stored in the registers are 100010100001 and 011100001011.
Convert the two binary numbers to hexadecimal numbers.

100010100001
011100001011

A toy store gives each of the toys it sells a unique code. The codes are 3-digit hexadecimal codes, for example A4B. A computer is used to store a database of the codes. The computer has 12-bit registers.

The hexadecimal code for each toy is stored in a barcode that is displayed on each toy. Each time a customer buys a toy, the barcode is scanned and the amount of stock stored in the database for that toy is reduced by 1. An item of hardware is used in this system to receive the hexadecimal code from the barcode scanner and find it in the database. The item of hardware is built into a single chip.

Identify the name of the item of hardware.

marking_points.text: Microprocessor

questions.text:
A toy store gives each of the toys it sells a unique code. The codes are 3-digit hexadecimal codes, for example A4B. A computer is used to store a database of the codes. The computer has 12-bit registers.

The hexadecimal code for each toy is stored in a barcode that is displayed on each toy. Each time a customer buys a toy, the barcode is scanned and the amount of stock stored in the database for that toy is reduced by 1.

Tick (✓) one box to show whether a barcode scanner is a type of input, output, process or storage device.

A input
B output
C process
D storage

marking_points.text: A

questions.text:
A toy store gives each of the toys it sells a unique code. The codes are 3-digit hexadecimal codes, for example A4B. A computer is used to store a database of the codes. The computer has 12-bit registers.

The hexadecimal code for each toy is stored in a barcode that is displayed on each toy. The barcode scanner contains a sensor.

Identify the type of sensor that the barcode scanner could contain.

marking_points.text: Light

questions.text:
A toy store gives each of the toys it sells a unique code. The codes are 3-digit hexadecimal codes, for example A4B. A computer is used to store a database of the codes. The computer has 12-bit registers.

The hexadecimal code for each toy is stored in a barcode that is displayed on each toy. A backup of the database is made at the end of each day using optical storage.

Describe how the database is written to the optical storage.

marking_points.text: The disk is spun
A laser is shone onto the disk
Pits are burnt into the disk
Pits are in a (spiral) track
Pits and lands represent binary values

questions.text:
Two binary numbers are stored in a computer.

Add the two binary numbers using binary addition. Give your answer in binary. Show all of your working.

  11110101
+ 00111001

answers.scoring_rule:
One mark for each correct nibble (2)
One mark for a correct method of working for example 4 or 5 correct carries
One mark for showing an overflow error

marking_points.text: One mark for each correct nibble: 0010 1110 (two marks for both nibbles correct)
One mark for a correct method of working for example 4 or 5 correct carries
One mark for showing an overflow error

questions.text:
Two binary numbers are stored in a computer.

A logic right shift of three places is performed on the binary number 11110101.

Give the denary number for the binary number that would be stored after the logical shift of three places has occurred. Show all of your working.

Denary number

marking_points.text: One mark for showing the binary number after the shift: 00011110
One mark for valid method of working to convert to denary: 16 + 8 + 4 + 2
One mark for correct denary number: 30

questions.text:
A programmer uses assembly language to create a program for a coffee machine.

Describe what is meant by assembly language.
questions.topic_name: Types of Programming Language, Translators and IDEs

marking_points.text: Low-level language
Uses mnemonics
Used to communicate directly with the computer hardware

questions.text:
A programmer uses assembly language to create a program for a coffee machine.

Identify the translator needed for assembly language.
questions.topic_name: Types of Programming Language, Translators and IDEs

marking_points.text: Assembler

questions.text:
A programmer uses assembly language to create a program for a coffee machine.

Tick (✓) one box to show which statement is an advantage of using assembly language.

A It is close to human language.
B It is memory efficient.
C It is easy to debug.
D It is machine independent.

rows: A It is close to human language.
C It is easy to debug.

questions.topic_name: Types of Programming Language, Translators and IDEs

marking_points.text: B

questions.text:
A computer with a Von Neumann architecture has a central processing unit (CPU) that performs the fetch–decode–execute (FDE) cycle.

Complete and annotate the diagram to show how instructions are decoded and executed.

control unit (CU)     arithmetic logic unit (ALU)
visuals: Partial diagram showing control unit (CU) and arithmetic logic unit (ALU) boxes
Partial diagram showing control unit (CU) and arithmetic logic unit (ALU) boxes
answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each correct part of the diagram.

marking_points.text: CIR is built into CU
CIR stores instruction
CU decodes the instruction
Instruction is decoded using an instruction set // Instruction is separated into opcode and operand
Signal is sent from CIR to ALU (to execute)
CU sends signal to RAM to fetch any data needed for instruction
ACC is built into ALU
ACC stores any data needed for instruction
ALU executes the instruction // ALU performs the mathematic/logical operation (needed by the instruction)

questions.text:
A computer with a Von Neumann architecture has a central processing unit (CPU) that performs the fetch–decode–execute (FDE) cycle. The CPU is dual core and has a processor speed of 2.4 GHz.

Describe what is meant by a core.

marking_points.text: Independent/self-contained processing unit
It executes/processes instructions // It performs the FDE cycle
It contains registers/units (to process data)

questions.text:
A computer with a Von Neumann architecture has a central processing unit (CPU) that performs the fetch–decode–execute (FDE) cycle.

Explain two ways that the performance of the CPU could be increased.

answers.scoring_rule: Four from (MAX 2 if no expansion given):

marking_points.text: The number of cores could be increased // by example …
… this means that more instructions can be processed simultaneously
The clock speed could be increased // by example …
… this means that more instructions can be processed (by a core) per second
The cache could be increased // by example …
… this means that more frequently used data/instructions can be stored (to be accessed faster than RAM)

questions.text:
A computer system has a mouse. An interrupt is sent to the CPU every time a button on the mouse is clicked.

Identify the name of this type of interrupt.

marking_points.text: Hardware (interrupt)

questions.text:
A computer system has a mouse. An interrupt is sent to the CPU every time a button on the mouse is clicked. The interrupt is placed in a queue.

Explain how the interrupt is serviced after it is placed in the queue.

marking_points.text: Checks the priority of the interrupt
If priority is higher, it halts the current process
… stores it on a stack
… fetches the interrupt
… checks the source of the interrupt
… calls the interrupt service routine (ISR) / interrupt handler to process the interrupt
If priority is not higher, it continues with current process

questions.text:
A computer system has a mouse. The mouse is connected using a universal serial bus (USB) connection.

Explain how data is sent using the USB connection.
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

marking_points.text: It is sent one bit at a time
It is sent down a single wire

questions.text:
A computer system has a mouse. The mouse is connected using a universal serial bus (USB) connection.

Give two benefits of using a USB connection for this purpose.

1
2
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

marking_points.text: The mouse can be powered by the device
It is a universal connection
It is backward compatible
Less chance of skewing // Less chance of errors
Supports different data transmission speeds
Adequate transmission speeds
The cable cannot be inserted incorrectly
The device is automatically detected // Driver is automatically installed when device is connected

questions.text:
A computer system has a mouse. The mouse is connected using a universal serial bus (USB) connection.

Give one drawback of using a USB connection for this purpose.
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

marking_points.text: Slower transmission than a parallel connection
Wire may get in the way // wire may limit movement of mouse

questions.text:
An online clothing company has a homepage at the uniform resource locator (URL) https://www.cieclothes.com/index.html

Write down the three different parts of the URL shown by the labels a, b and c.

https://www.cieclothes.com/index.html
             a              b          c

a [blank]   b [blank]   c [blank]
questions.topic_name: The Internet and the World Wide Web

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each correct label

marking_points.text: a: Protocol
b: Domain name
c: Web page name // file name

questions.text:
An online clothing company has a homepage at the uniform resource locator (URL) https://www.cieclothes.com/index.html

The web pages are stored on a web server. A proxy server is used to stop distributed denial of service (DDoS) attacks on the web server.

Describe how the proxy server can stop a DDoS attack reaching a web server.

marking_points.text: It examines each request/incoming transmission
It can slow down the rate at which/limits the number of requests sent to the server
It can use caching …
… to respond to the request (rather than forwarding it to the server)
It can stop any requests from a certain IP

questions.text:
An online clothing company has a homepage at the uniform resource locator (URL) https://www.cieclothes.com/index.html. Customers use the website to buy clothing from the company.

Complete the table with the missing terms and descriptions about retrieving and displaying web pages.

Term | Description
hypertext markup language (HTML) | [blank]
domain name server (DNS) | [blank]
[blank] | It is a numerical address used to locate the web server.
[blank] | It is a secure protocol that can be used to transmit data sent to and received from a web server.
[blank] | It is a piece of software that is used to display web pages.

visuals.description: Two-column table with Term and Description headers; some cells pre-filled and others blank

rows.definition: It is a numerical address used to locate the web server.
It is a secure protocol that can be used to transmit data sent to and received from a web server.
It is a piece of software that is used to display web pages.

questions.topic_name: The Internet and the World Wide Web

visuals.description: Two-column table with Term and Description headers; some cells pre-filled and others blank

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each correct missing term or definition.

marking_points.text: hypertext markup language (HTML): It is the language that is often used to write/author web pages.

domain name server (DNS): It is a system that stores URLs/domain names and their matching/corresponding IP addresses // It is a system that provides the matching IP address for a webpage to the web browser.
IP address: It is a numerical address used to locate the web server.
SSL/TLS/HTTPS: It is a secure protocol that can be used to transmit data sent to and from a web server.
web browser: It is a piece of software that is used to display web pages.

questions.text:
An online clothing company has a homepage at the uniform resource locator (URL) https://www.cieclothes.com/index.html. The clothing company uses cookies to store customer details.

Complete the statements about cookies.
Use terms from the list. Not all terms need to be used. You should only use a term once.

asymmetric   DNS   dynamic   persistent   session   static   symmetric   user   utility software   web browser

Cookies are stored and managed by the [blank] .

[blank] cookies are temporary files that are created when a user visits a web page.

[blank] cookies are permanent files that are created when a user visits a web page.
questions.topic_name: The Internet and the World Wide Web

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each correct missing term in the correct place.

marking_points.text: Cookies are stored and managed by the web browser.
Session cookies are temporary files that are created when a user visits a web page.
Persistent cookies are permanent files that are created when a user visits a web page.

questions.text:
A house has a smart speaker that can take voice commands as input. A person in the house can tell the smart speaker to play music or audio books.

The smart speaker cannot be described as a robot.

Explain why the smart speaker is not a robot.

marking_points.text: It does not have a mechanical structure
It does not have any actuators
… so it cannot move itself

questions.text:
A house has a smart speaker that can take voice commands as input. A person in the house can tell the smart speaker to play music or audio books.

Identify the input device that allows voice commands to be used as input.

marking_points.text: Microphone

questions.text:
A house has a smart speaker that can take voice commands as input. A person in the house can tell the smart speaker to play music or audio books. The smart speaker has machine learning capabilities.

Explain how the smart speaker can use machine learning to improve the voice command input.

marking_points.text: Gather data from the same/different voices // It can ask a user to say lots of different words
Analyse/identify patterns in a user's voice
It can adapt its own processes
It can learn to identify a user's voice
Stores successful/unsuccessful voice commands
It can learn different ways of making the same request
It can learn the different pronunciations // It can learn different accents
It can learn by listening to lots of different voices
It can learn to ignore any background/environmental noise if a user is speaking

questions.text:
A house has a smart speaker that can take voice commands as input. A person in the house can tell the smart speaker to play music or audio books. The smart speaker is an example of an embedded system.

Explain why the smart speaker is an embedded system.

marking_points.text: It has a single/limited function
It has dedicated hardware
It has a microprocessor
Its function is not easily changed

questions.text:
A house has a smart speaker that can take voice commands as input. A person in the house can tell the smart speaker to play music or audio books. The music and audio books that are played by the smart speaker are stored in cloud storage.

Give two advantages of storing the music and audio books in cloud storage.

1
2

marking_points.text: The music/books can be easily accessed from any smart speaker
The user doesn't need to purchase a secondary storage device for the books/music // Smart speaker doesn't need a large amount of secondary storage
The user doesn't need to maintain the storage needed for the books/music
Smart speaker can be smaller in size (as it doesn't need large amounts of secondary storage)
Almost unlimited storage available for the books/music // Easy to increase storage capacity needed

questions.text:
A house has a smart speaker that can take voice commands as input. A person in the house can tell the smart speaker to play music or audio books. The data for the music and audio books needs to be checked for errors after being transmitted from the cloud.

Explain how errors may occur in the data during transmission.

marking_points.text: Data could be lost
Data could be gained
Data could be changed
Skewing of data could occur
Interference could occur
Crosstalk could occur
Data collisions could occur
Data packets could time out/reach their hop count

questions.text:
A house has a smart speaker that can take voice commands as input. A person in the house can tell the smart speaker to play music or audio books. The data for the music and audio books needs to be checked for errors after being transmitted from the cloud.

Give two error detection methods that could be used to check for errors in the data after transmission.

1
2

marking_points.text: Parity byte check
Parity block check
Checksum
Echo check
ARQ
